question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with limit=1 and repeatedly poll the endpoint in a tight loop, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute many small DB queries that collectively exhaust the DB connection pool and statement timeout budget, causing a non-network DoS for other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/<96-char-hex> (Hedera hash format), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute `HASH like '<96-char-prefix>%'`, and if the hash column index does not support prefix LIKE efficiently, force a sequential scan of the entire record_file table, exhausting DB I/O and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=eq:9999999999999 (a number that does not exist), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a query that returns no rows but still performs a full index scan, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with both block.number and timestamp filters simultaneously, causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a WHERE clause with conditions on both index and consensus_end columns, and if the DB query planner chooses a suboptimal plan, exhaust DB CPU and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with order=desc and no other filters, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute `ORDER BY consensus_end DESC LIMIT <defaultLimit>` without a WHERE clause, and if the record_file table is large and lacks a proper index, exhaust DB resources and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockViewModel constructor] Can an unprivileged external user trigger GET /blocks returning rows where recordFile.hash is null or undefined, causing addHexPrefix(recordFile.hash) in BlockViewModel to throw a TypeError that propagates as an unhandled exception, crashing the request and potentially the process, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockViewModel constructor] Can an unprivileged external user trigger GET /blocks returning rows where recordFile.consensusStart or recordFile.consensusEnd is null, causing nsToSecNs() in BlockViewModel to throw or return unexpected values, leading to a serialization error that crashes the response pipeline and causes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=gte:1.000000000&timestamp=lte:1.000000001 (a 1-nanosecond range), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a query with an extremely narrow timestamp range that still requires a full index scan to confirm no rows exist, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/1 (a valid block number), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute `INDEX = $1` and return a row with a large logsBloom value, and then BlockViewModel to call toHexString(recordFile.logsBloom, true, 512) which allocates a 1024-character string, and if many such requests are made concurrently, exhaust Node.js heap memory and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gt:0 and limit=100, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to return 100 rows each with a large bytes column, and the `coalesce(size, length(bytes))` expression to decompress TOAST data for each row, exhausting DB I/O and memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildAndValidateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with a query string containing many unknown parameters (e.g., ?a=1&b=2&...&z=26), causing buildAndValidateFilters() to iterate over all parameters and collect them as unknownParams, and if the error message construction for InvalidArgumentError.forRequestValidation() is expensive, exhaust CPU and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=ne:0 (not-equal operator), and if the filter is accepted and passed to the DB as `index != 0`, cause a full table scan returning all blocks except block 0, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractOrderFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with multiple order= parameters (e.g., order=asc&order=desc&order=asc), causing extractOrderFromFilters() to use findLast() to pick the last value, and if the repeated parameter processing in buildAndValidateFilters() is not rate-limited, exhaust CPU processing many order values and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/0x<64-char-hex> where the hash prefix matches many rows in the record_file table (e.g., all hashes starting with '00'), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute `HASH like '00%'` and scan many rows before returning the first match via getSingleRow(), exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=gt:0 and order=asc, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a forward scan of the entire record_file table ordered by consensus_end ascending, and if the table has millions of rows, exhaust DB I/O and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gte:0 (matching all blocks), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a query with `index >= 0` and the default limit, and if the index column scan is expensive due to table bloat, exhaust DB resources and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/0x followed by exactly 64 hex characters (valid ETH hash format), causing validateHashOrNumber() to call isValidEthHashOrHederaHash() which applies the regex `/^(0x)?([0-9A-Fa-f]{64}|[0-9A-Fa-f]{96})$/`, and if the regex engine has catastrophic backtracking on certain inputs, block the event loop and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with limit=<maxLimit> and no WHERE filters, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to return the maximum number of rows, each requiring BlockViewModel instantiation with addHexPrefix(), nsToSecNs(), and toHexString() calls, exhausting Node.js CPU and memory during response serialization and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=lte:9999999999 and order=desc, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute `WHERE index <= 9999999999 ORDER BY consensus_end DESC LIMIT <default>`, and if the orderBy column (consensus_end) differs from the WHERE column (index), force the DB to perform a sort operation on a large result set, exhausting DB memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=lte:99999999999.999999999 (far future timestamp), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute `WHERE consensus_end <= <large_value>` matching all rows, and with the default limit still force a full index scan, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/<number> where number is a valid block number that exists, causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to return a row with a very large bytes column, and the response serialization to exhaust Node.js memory, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gt:0&block.number=gt:1&block.number=gt:2 (many repeated block.number filters up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters), causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a WHERE clause with many redundant conditions, forcing the DB query planner to evaluate all conditions and exhausting DB CPU, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=gte:0&timestamp=gte:1&timestamp=gte:2 (many repeated timestamp filters), causing buildWhereSqlStatement() to build a WHERE clause with many AND conditions on consensus_end, forcing the DB to evaluate all conditions and exhausting DB CPU, causing a non-network DoS?
